thread.title: Union Minister, Mayor to inaugurate 19 municipal school buildings in Surat
thread.title_full: Union Minister, Mayor to inaugurate 19 municipal school buildings in Surat

author: @indianexpress
title: Union Minister, Mayor to inaugurate 19 municipal school buildings in Surat
text:
skip to main content Sections English தமிழ் বাংলা മലയാളം ગુજરાતી हिंदी मराठी Business बिज़नेस Newsletters Edition India International Friday, Nov 22, 2024 ePaper Today’s Paper Journalism of Courage Home ePaper My Express UPSC World India Explained Opinion Politics Entertainment Business Sports Cities Lifestyle Tech Research Videos Subscribe Sign In Trending Premium at ₹99 / mo Elections Express Shorts Mini Crossword Premium 🎙️ Podcast Health & Wellness Advertisement News Cities Ahmedabad Union Minister, Mayor to inaugurate 19 municipal school buildings in Surat Union Minister, Mayor to inaugurate 19 municipal school buildings in Surat The new buildings will be multi-storied ones, compared to the traditional single-storey structure, to accommodate more students. By: Express News Service Ahmedabad | November 23, 2024 04:37 IST 2 min read CR Paatil (Photo: CR Paatil/ Facebook)
In the presence of Union Jal Shakti Minister C R Paatil, Surat Mayor Daxesh Mawani will lay the foundation stone for construction of new buildings of 19 primary schools, being built at a cost of Rs 45.20 crore, in Surat city and district on Sunday.
The new buildings will be multi-storied ones, compared to the traditional single-storey structure, to accommodate more students. The areas where the new schools will come up are Rander zone with five schools; Udhna A and Katargam with four schools each; Varachha B zone with two schools; and Varachha A zone, Athwa, Udhna B and Central Zone with one school each.
Sources in Surat Municipal Corporation (SMC) said that once the schools get ready, more students will get the benefit of studying in Gujarati, Marathi, Hindi, Urdu, Odia, and English mediums.
Advertisement The sources further said the SMC had sent a proposal of 19 schools, which are over 20 to 40 years old, with their structure weak and posing risk to students, to the state government. The proposal was approved and grants allotted under Samagra Siksha.
Administrative officer of Municipal School Board, Surat, Mehul Patel said, “Presently, there are over 1.91 lakh students studying in 359 schools in Surat. The work on 19 schools will be completed before next Diwali. For now, students of these schools have been shifted to other nearby municipal schools…This year, we have 15,000 additional students. Many of the students have moved from private schools to municipal schools in Surat.”
Patel said, the tender work for 20 more schools is in process. “It will be completed by end of next year,” he added.

They have discussed resuming direct flights and media exchanges, but there are differences on issues like Kailash Mansarovar Yatra and river data-sharing. India has agreed to continue talks, including de-escalation of troops along the Line of Actual Control.
